retrieved.title: Web services
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

retrieved.title: Additional bachelor/diploma programme
retrieved.text:
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. The admission does not ensue automatically. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/933) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to be admitted to. After a few working days, the new degree programme will be visible in u:space > Study overview. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
retrieved.text: ## / - Step 1: Check your record of examinations and/or semester planner You can find the record of examinations in u:space under the tab “Studies”. It is organised according to the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which courses you have already completed, which achievements you have to complete next and what requirements you have to fulfil for them. You can find the semester planner in u:space under the tab “Courses”. It shows you the courses currently offered and provides you with a calendar to help you plan your semester. Attention: Please always check the [curriculum of your degree programme](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula). The record of examinations in u:space supports you in planning, but you have to select the correct curriculum point yourself on the basis of your curriculum when registering for the course or exam - Step 2: Select an achievement from your curriculum Search for modules for which you still have to complete courses (red and orange traffic light symbol) in your record of examinations in u:space. After that with the help of the record of examinations and by looking at your [curriculum](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula), determine for which curriculum point (module) you are still missing a course / exam. To find out which courses you can attend for the curriculum point you have not yet completed, either click on "Link to u:find" at the book icon

retrieved.title: Confirmations
retrieved.text:
Current students of the University of Vienna can download most confirmations themselves in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) at Personal matters > My documents at any time. For confirmations issued in English you first switch the language u:space is displayed in to English. These confirmations carry an official electronic signature and are accepted at official authorities.

External institutions, parents and third parties only receive confirmations if they can provide an authorisation (power of attorney) and a copy of the personal ID of the person concerned.

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

heading_path: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
retrieved.text: u:ai is operated in a secure cloud where all data is processed within the EU. The content you enter is not used for training AI models. This means that any text, documents, or questions you enter into u:ai remain in a protected environment. Bei vielen kommerziellen KI-Tools ist hingegen unklar, wo Daten gespeichert werden, wie lange sie aufbewahrt bleiben und ob sie für Trainingszwecke weiterverwendet werden. Daraus ergibt sich ein erhöhtes Risiko, insbesondere bei der Arbeit mit personenbezogenen, sensiblen oder studienrelevanten Inhalten What does this mean for me? - u:ai is compliant with data protection requirements insofar as your personal data and the information you enter are processed in accordance with the data protection standards of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This does not apply if, within the same chat, you use the web search function or the Mistral model. - When processing the personal data of third parties, the principle of data minimisation applies as a general rule. This means that only the most essential personal data may be collected, processed and stored, in order to achive a specific defined objective. - In order to process the personal data of third parties or research data, you must obtain the consent of the persons concerned. In this context, please refer to the [information on handling research data](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/z_ctl_neu/Angebote_fuer_Studierende/Handouts__Leitfaeden_und_Ressourcen/Handouts_und_Leitfaeden/2024_09_16_AI_and_research_data_EN.pdf). - **[Sensitive data](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGJDSrGw=/?moveToWidget=3458764658613096304&cot=14)** (this is a category of personal data that is

retrieved.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
retrieved.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

retrieved.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai is the University of Vienna's AI platform, providing students with data protection-compliant access to various AI models. The platform was designed specifically for use in a university context and is part of the Austria-wide Academic AI project.

u:ai can support you in your learning, writing, and analysis—you always retain responsibility for the subject matter and control over the content.

#

u:ai is the University of Vienna's official access point to AI models such as ChatGPT in a secure cloud environment. This means:

- All data is processed within the EU (exception: Internet search, Mistral model)
- The content entered will not be used for training AI models.

However, U:ai was not trained with university-specific content. The AI therefore does not have internal knowledge about courses, exams, or internal institute regulations.

heading_path: Why should I use u:ai? Other AI tools can do so much more!
reranked.text: u:ai is operated in a secure cloud where all data is processed within the EU. The content you enter is not used for training AI models. Bei vielen kommerziellen KI-Tools ist hingegen unklar, wo Daten gespeichert werden, wie lange sie aufbewahrt bleiben und ob sie für Trainingszwecke weiterverwendet werden. This does not apply if, within the same chat, you use the web search function or the Mistral model. - When processing the personal data of third parties, the principle of data minimisation applies as a general rule. This means that only the most essential personal data may be collected, processed and stored, in order to achive a specific defined objective. - In order to process the personal data of third parties or research data, you must obtain the consent of the persons concerned. In this context, please refer to the [information on handling research data](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/z_ctl_neu/Angebote_fuer_Studierende/Handouts__Leitfaeden_und_Ressourcen/Handouts_und_Leitfaeden/2024_09_16_AI_and_research_data_EN.pdf). - **[Sensitive data](https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVGJDSrGw=/?moveToWidget=3458764658613096304&cot=14)** (this is a category of personal data that is

reranked.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
reranked.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
u:ai enables the creation of so-called tailored AIs. These are individually configured chatbots that permanently store your instructions.

- Such chatbots can optionally work with their own knowledge base (e.g., guidelines, templates), which is then always given priority. This significantly reduces [hallucinations](/en/using-ai-in-your-studies/what-is-artificial-intelligence/#c1268300).
- Once you have built a chatbot, you can also share it with other people.

This means that recurring tasks can be handled more efficiently without having to explain what the AI should do each time.

In certain modules, an Internet search can be activated as an option. This accesses external information via the [Perplexity](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perplexity.ai) service. This gives you access to up-to-date information.

When Internet search is activated, the entire chat history to date is used as context. This means that personal or confidential data used in this chat leaves the closed system of u:ai. Data protection compliance is then no longer fully guaranteed.

Therefore, never use personal or confidential content in the same chat as the Internet search, but start a new chat for the Internet search.

When using AI in higher education, it is not only functionality that plays a role, but above all the legal and data protection framework in which a tool is operated. U:ai was developed specifically for the university context and differs in key respects from freely accessible, commercial AI offerings.

reranked.title: u:ai - The AI tool of the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
To access u:ai, you need your u:account. Access is via [univie.academic-ai.at](https://univie.academic-ai.at)

You have a monthly credit of €20.00 available for queries. The cost of a specific query depends on the length of the question and the length of the answer (file uploads cost more than text queries, shorter texts less than long texts). You can view and monitor your credit balance directly in u:ai. Once the amount has been used up, you will not be able to use u:ai again until the following month.

Since the monthly credit is limited, u:ai should be used specifically for study and university-related questions. The credit is automatically recharged on the first day of each month. Unused credit cannot be carried over to the following month.

**Knowledge Cut-Off:**

U:ai is currently based on information that was available until October 2024. The system does not know anything that happened after that. To incorporate up-to-date information, you must activate the [Internet search](#c1268815).

[1] Web services
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

[2] Student Space: room for students
- [Gender neutral toilet facilities (All gender toilets)](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/all-gender-toilets/)
- [Quiet Boxes](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/quiet-boxes/)
- [Dispenser for menstrual products](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/dispenser-for-menstrual-products/)
- [SWITCH Powerbanks for studying](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/switch-powerbanks-for-studying/)
- [Baby changing tables](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/student-space/baby-changing-tables/)

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

[3] Additional bachelor/diploma programme — Further bachelor/diploma programmes > Non-EU/EEA secondary-school leaving certificate
1. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to study additionally within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
2. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
3. The admission does not ensue automatically.
